evidence.scientific_clarity: Two-panel ROC+PRC layout clearly shows rank ordering of 4 classifiers; diagonal and prevalence baselines present; AUROC/AP values in legend match brief requirements.
evidence.typography: Script sets neither pdf.fonttype=42 nor svg.fonttype='none'; fonts embedded as Type 3 (bitmap-subset, not editable TrueType); DejaVu Sans default used—no custom font choice.
evidence.palette: Gray/blue/green/red palette is largely colorblind-problematic (green+red together); however distinct linestyles partially mitigate; green reserved for Method-B not directional meaning.
evidence.layout_density: figsize=(9,4.2) with tight subplots_adjust; curves fill axes well; no gaping whitespace; legend placed in lower-right (ROC) and upper-right (PRC) without overlap.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content and legend labels; legend includes AUROC/AP values inline; minor issue: legend contains duplicate 'Random'/'Prevalence baseline' entry due to double-append in code.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF exists and is Matplotlib-generated, but fonts are Type 3 (not Type 42/TrueType); text is NOT selectable/editable; fonttype=42 was not set in the script.
evidence.file_format: All four required files present: figure.pdf, figure.png, caption.tex, gen_figure.py; additionally figure.svg generated; all non-trivial.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption states specific AUROC and AP values for all four methods, identifies OursModel as best (0.989/0.984), mentions prevalence baseline value (P=0.40), and notes data source (data.json, no mocking).

overall_strengths: Clear two-panel design with all required reference lines (random diagonal, prevalence baseline) and per-method AUROC/AP annotations in the legend directly address the brief.
Caption is publication-quality: bold title, numerical claims for all methods, specific prevalence value, and provenance statement about raw scores.
overall_weaknesses: Font type is Type 3 (bitmap subset) rather than Type 42 (editable TrueType); pdf.fonttype=42 was never set, making text non-selectable in the PDF and failing vector-fidelity requirements.

Palette uses green and red as categorical method colors, which conflicts with the convention that green/red signal directionality; combined with missing colorblind-safe palette (Okabe-Ito or similar), accessibility is reduced.
